frameworks.name: Bayesian structure learning
frameworks.one_line: Observers partition outcomes into latent contexts, each with its own expected outcome.

frameworks.name: reference-dependent preferences
frameworks.one_line: Reference points derived from context-specific expectations rather than a single global one.

frameworks.name: prospect theory
frameworks.one_line: Risk-aversion above and risk-seeking below a reference point motivates the question.

core_question: Where do the reference points used in reference-dependent risky choice come from?
key_assumptions: Reference points correspond to expected outcomes
Observers can infer latent contexts that partition outcome distributions
Context inference follows Bayesian structure learning
manipulations: Abrupt versus gradual change in the distribution of outcomes
Distribution shifts across blocks of a risky-choice task
key_findings: Dramatic distribution change induces discovery of a new reference point
New reference points produce systematic shifts in risk preferences
Gradual changes update a single reference point continuously without partitioning
interpretations: Reference points are emergent products of Bayesian context inference, not fixed or single global expectations
